key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration state prediction across fundamentally different welding processes, such as TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: The approach reduces the need for extensive relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring to new process types, improving practical scalability across welding systems.
method: Train a UDA model to learn domain-invariant penetration features.
Use gradual source domain expansion to adapt more robustly across process shifts.
Validate on both same-process and cross-process TIG/laser transfer tasks.
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG accuracy in cross-process transfer, outperforming the supervised baseline by about 35–43% absolute.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on the similarity and coverage of source-domain welding states, so deployment on new materials or parameter regimes may require careful calibration.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
